text: Planning my trip to Vietnam took weeks of research, but the experience exceeded every expectation. Immersing myself in a new culture felt both exciting and humbling. The street food was unforgettable—I tried pho from a tiny stall and spicy noodles that left my mouth tingling. Language barriers led to funny moments; once, I accidentally ordered dessert instead of dinner using hand gestures. Despite these challenges, locals were patient and kind. Now, those memories—the chaotic markets, the kindness of strangers, and the taste of real Vietnamese coffee—are treasures I carry with me. Travel truly transforms how you see the world.
